mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. If payrolls rise but returns are negative over a majority of periods, the hypothesis fails.
failure_condition: If payrolls increase over a 12-month rolling window but S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative more than 60% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 3.5% indicates a tightening cycle that raises the discount rate for equities, leading to lower valuations and negative forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 3.5%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in retail sales and payrolls signals robust demand-driven growth that may overheat the economy, prompting anticipated Fed tightening and thus negative equity returns over the subsequent 20 trading days. The interaction is active when both series exceed their 12-month moving averages.
candidate_trigger: Both RSAFS and PAYEMS exceed their 12-month moving averages
failure_condition: If joint exceedance of 12-month moving averages leads to positive 20-day returns more often than negative, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime where CPIAUCSL exceeds 200, rising federal funds rates aggressively combat inflation, severely compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 40% of occasions when CPIAUCSL > 200 and FEDFUNDS > 3%, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects increasing corporate output and economic momentum, which supports higher earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If INDPRO increases over a 3-month period but 20-day forward returns are negative more than 55% of the time, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts above 2 million units annually signal robust construction activity and consumer confidence, leading to strong economic growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: If 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of episodes where HOUST > 2000, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in both the federal funds rate and the consumer price index indicates that the central bank is tightening into high inflation, which historically depresses equity prices over the next month as real rates rise.
failure_condition: If the joint condition produces positive 20-day returns in a majority of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A high unemployment rate above 5% signals slack in the labor market, which historically precedes accommodative monetary policy and mean-reverting recovery that leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of episodes with UNRATE > 5.0%, the hypothesis fails.
